relationship_decisions.rationale: the owner is the sole technical founder of Example Forecast Lab — the founding relationship is well-evidenced and structurally important.
the owner is Principal & Founder of Example Studio since 2021 — the founding relationship is well-evidenced and central to his identity.
the owner held the Director of Product Design role at Example HR for 6 years — a formative career relationship worth preserving.
the owner co-founded Example Care — a category-defining co-founding relationship with documented outcomes.
the owner was Head of Design at Example Arts Festival — a significant early career role with documented impact.
the owner co-founded Example Audio — a durable co-founding relationship worth preserving in the career graph.
the owner worked on Example Labs dashboards — a background project with clear ownership signal from upload evidence.
the owner worked on Example Events in 2013 — evidenced in the canonical bio upload.
the owner led the Data team at Example Fitness — evidenced in the canonical bio upload.
Example Data Studio was a Example Studio client engagement — the studio-to-engagement relationship is structurally important for tracing the embedded partner model.
Example Care Journal was built under Example Studio — preserving this link connects the conversational AI work to the studio operating model.
Example Market was a co-founder/Example Studio engagement — preserves the studio's portfolio scope.
Example Wellness was a Example Studio engagement — preserves the studio's brand/wellness portfolio work.
Example Logistics was a Example Studio engagement — demonstrates the embedded design-engineering partnership playbook in practice.
Example Golf was a Example Studio engagement — preserves the studio's sports/commerce portfolio work.
The senior-builder-plus-AI model defines Example Studio's operating model — this relationship is the conceptual backbone of the studio identity.
Design-engineering compression enables the senior-builder-plus-AI model — a durable causal relationship between the foundational concept and operating model.
Example Forecast Lab implements three-window temporal validation — the implementation relationship connects the live product to its proprietary methodology.
Example Data Studio demonstrated AI-assisted velocity coaching concretely (50→300+ commits/week) — the primary evidence base for the concept.
Example Data Studio produced a 66-component design system using semantic tokens — a primary concrete demonstration of the concept.
Example Logistics rebuilt its design system with semantic tokens and 1:1 Figma-to-code mapping — a second strong demonstration of the concept.
Example Care Journal implements the conversational AI clinical arc — the direct application relationship between project and framework.
Example Forecast Lab is the primary live demonstration of the zero-to-one AI product build playbook — preserving this surfaces the playbook's strongest evidence.
Example Care Journal is a secondary demonstration of the zero-to-one playbook — 60,000 waitlist as sole designer-engineer reinforces the playbook's applicability.
Example Data Studio is the primary demonstration of the embedded design-engineering partnership playbook.
Example Logistics is a secondary demonstration of the embedded design-engineering partnership playbook — onboarding compression from 2 months to 2 weeks.
Software as an architectural problem is the foundational philosophical stance of Example Studio — grounds the studio's intellectual identity.
The unified discipline stance directly reinforces design-engineering compression — brand/architecture/interface as one discipline is the philosophical expression of the compression concept.
Small-studio-by-conviction is the philosophical grounding for the senior-builder-plus-AI operating model — the stance and model are conceptually inseparable.
The prediction-engine IP path inquiry concerns the future of IP currently housed in Example Forecast Lab — a direct strategic concern relationship.
The inquiry specifically calls out the factor-discovery + temporal-validation methodology as the IP asset — connects the open question directly to the underlying concept.
The studio scale ceiling inquiry directly questions the limits of the senior-builder-plus-AI model — the relationship is the core of the inquiry.
Example Forecast Lab uses LangGraph and multi-model routing — agentic architecture maturity is directly relevant to this active project.
Example Care Journal uses OpenAI Agent Kit — agentic architecture maturity is directly relevant to this active project.

notes: All 29 node proposals are assigned 'create' because the existing graph contains only Brain system starter pages (brain, projects, stances, concepts, playbooks, inquiries, current-focus, inbox-intake-flow, source-to-atom-promotion, contradiction-review, how-should-the-system-evolve, local-first-knowledge-should-stay-file-first, starter-graph, relationship-map, channels, companies, people). None of these are substantively related to Example Owner's projects, concepts, stances, or inquiries.
All vector match scores across all 29 proposals are below 0.35 and in every case the top match is a generic Brain infrastructure page, not a domain-equivalent node. No merge or update is warranted.

All 34 edge proposals are kept. Every relationship is grounded in direct evidence from the input bundle and represents a meaningful semantic connection between newly created nodes. No edges reference existing Brain system pages as source or target.

The 'ent-028 concerns ent-002' edge from the proposal artifact was not listed as a formal edge_proposal in the bundle but is implied by the inquiry's relates_to_refs. It is omitted from relationship_decisions as it was not formally proposed; it should be added during write.

The two open inquiries not entityized (how-should-example-forecast-lab-evolve and where-is-the-next-frontier-for-example-studio-engagements) remain non-nodes per the synthesis artifact's treatment and are not addressed here.
